reviews.text: They do not return emails and my lawyer quit half way through my case to work at the bankruptcy courts.  Always felt like she was never on our side. The new lawyer assigned to us has never contacted us and now the paralegal is not returning our emails.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Wendy - you filed a chapter 13 case with our office over 2 years ago on 8-2-2017. A chapter 13 is a debt repayment plant that involves preparing the bankruptcy documents, attending a creditor's meeting, working with the chapter 13 trustee's office to resolve any objections to confirmation, and attending confirmation hearings with the court. We handled all of these steps, all the trustee objections were resolved and the plan was ready for to be confirmed nearly two years ago on 01-30-2018. Your case was successfully confirmed by the court on 04-09-2018. After the plan is confirmed, your case primarily involves you making your monthly plan payments. In other words, there is little to no legal work involved after your case has been confirmed.

While it is incorrect, we can understand you feeling that you "felt like she (the attorney) was never on our side." You can have this feeling when what you want runs contrary to the bankruptcy rules to get your plan confirmed. While we sympathize with your feelings, we are often placed in the position of being the bearer of bad news. We hope you recognize that we are on your side, but we have to work within the parameters of the bankruptcy code.

As for not responding to a recent email--during the repayment period of the plan--if you sent emails in the first couple of weeks of September of this year, we experienced intermittent email disruption with our hosting provider. We have since switched email hosting providers and haven't had any reported issues since making the change. In any event, if you haven't received a response from one or more emails, we encourage you to call us. Our number is 801-285-6866. By calling in after a non-responsive email, we can avoid miscommunication especially when a staff member is out of the office or has overlooked the email.

reviews.text: I did not hire this company, I found them to have the highest price in the valley. Also, if you write negative reviews they will threaten you. The following is a quote from Russell Weekes correspondence with me "Be advised that since you have refused to remove the review, you have waived your attorney-client privilege and we can now publish information regarding your consultation in order to defend ourselves and take other appropriate action against you."
reviews.ownerResponse:
Admittedly we are not the cheapest bankruptcy law firm available: we are very experienced and are not bottom basement bankruptcy attorneys looking to gain experience. However, we are not the most expensive either as you assert. We are one of the TOP bankruptcy law firms in the State. We would not be able to file more cases than other bankruptcy firms if we were the "highest price" and our prices were not commensurate
with our experience and expertise compared to the market. Capstone Law offers moderately priced and flexible payment options to make the cost of filing bankruptcy affordable and feasible to match our experienced and competent bankruptcy attorneys. Interested parties can read more about what our actual clients say about us at https://www.weekes-law.com/.

A few years back, I got a consultation from Jeremy and he promised me a clean slate and a fresh start. All I needed to do was come up with the fifteen hundred dollar retainer fee. I worked and I saved it up and paid him.  He looked into my case and told me that i w a s making too much money to file bankruptcy. He kept my money and did nothing else for me. Shortly after that, I gave him the same rating im giving him now. He promptly contacted me and asked why and what he could do to improve. I hoped he would help me in some way at the least if I deleted the negative rating. So I did. He thanked me for deleting it, by email, and then again did nothing else. Be very careful with your money. Hiring him as my bankruptcy attorney only made my situation worse. Just like all the others he took advantage of someone in a time of weakness.

Once I realized that I was not going to be able to keep up with everything do to my business failing. I also realized I was most likely going to have to go for bankruptcy. I was very stressed, and uncertain as I did not know anything about Bankruptcy. I googled "Bankruptcy Attorneys". And one of the first names that came up was Jeremy McCullough. So I decided to give his office a call. (In all the tragedy of losing my business, and everything I owned. The fact that I could find someone as nice and professional. As Jeremy, is something I will always be grateful for. He was always helpful, and I could see immediately he was honest.) I called, they were able to set up my free consultation in a timely manor. He explained everything in away that was easy to understand. I immediately began to feel a lot better. Once I had put down the money for my retainer, everything was a stress free simple process. In the next few weeks that followed all my creditors called me. I was able to say that I was in bankruptcy, and this was my attorneys phone number. AND THEY NEVER CALLED AGAIN!  (He offered a payment plan.) But most importantly he was just a phone call away. If I had any questions along the way, he was always able to answer them in a professional way. His office staff was very helpful, and very nice. The overall price was very reasonable. Again I just want to say Thanks to Mr. McCullough, and all of his office staff. For helping me through a very difficult time in my life. The best news is the bankruptcy is closed now. (Also Mr. McCullough helps you get in touch with classes that help you learn how to manage your money. To help you live a stress free lifestyle.)

He is very knowledgeable and most importantly i have found him patient, which can be a rare quality in any profession.

The thought of applying for disability never entered my mind, until life took an unexpected turn. Scared, frustrated and deeply distressed about where to turn for help in even starting a disability process.  I stumbled across Barnes Disability Advocates while googling.

I am so very glad I choose to make an appointment.  From my first consultation to multiple calls and questions throughout the process, everyone  was so very kind and patient. They guided me through the complex paperwork with extensive knowledge and professionalism. Even though, I am sure, many of my questions were basic or repetitive.

Disability changed my life forever. However, with the help and guidance that everyone at Barnes Disability Advocates provided, I had one less stress while adjusting to this new reality and phase of my life, and that has made all the difference.

To EVERYONE at Barnes Disability Advocates - Thank you, from the bottom of my heart.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you! We always appreciate hearing how much our work has helped.

The Barnes group has been amazing. They have EXCELLENT communication skills, research teams as well as well qualified Attorneys on staff that got the job done. They continue to work with me on finalizing all steps in the process and I am grateful for them being able to bring closure to this over 3 year battle in my life. Set back after setback the Barnes group was right there on the front lines to see things through. I can’t thank them enough especially Erin and her follow though. Erin is an amazing asset to the organization and enough praise cannot be put into words to how meaningful her work on my case was. Todd Renda was also an amazing attorney ready to go to bat for what he knew was right. Thank you to everyone involved,… I would not hesitate to make a recommendation for their services without reservation in the slightest.

Thank you Barnes Disability for everything you’ve done, now hopefully I can begin to rebuild,…

Ryan Frei (actual Branes client)

reviews.text: Barnes Disability Advocates have given me hope. The people are caring, patient, empathetic, and thorough. I have less stress in my life because of Barnes. They really know what they're doing!

I was referred to Attorney Matt Ormsby and the Jay Barnes Disability Law Firm in the Summer of 2023 from another attorney who represented me on another matter. From the first call until my Social Security Disability was approved on October 1, 2024, everyone in the firm always treated me like I was the most important client that they had. I met with the Social Security Doctor on Saturday August 31st, the beginning of Labor Day Weekend, and on Sunday, Jay Barnes contacted me regarding how everything went. I thought it went quite well and apparently it did, since my next contact was my approval, without a hearing before an ALJ. My experience with the Jay Barnes firm ranked as one of the most pleasant and successful business endeavors of my life. Brett P.

I had an excellent experience with Barnes Disability Advocates handling my SSDI case. They guided me through the process with patience and kindness, always ensuring I understood each step along the way. Their communication was outstanding, and I felt fully supported throughout the entire process. Thanks to their hard work and dedication, my case was brought to a successful close. I highly recommend them to anyone seeking professional and compassionate help with their disability claims!

I have referred many of my friends and family to these wonderful people and they have all received the best of care.  These are the best.

On two separate occasions, I was forced to terminate attorneys involved in my matter based on the advice of my business advisors. In one instance, an attorney exercised power/abuse of power in written communication in a manner that raised serious concerns within my entire business team, resulting in a unified recommendation to disengage from the firm altogether.

In addition, responsiveness was poor and there were significant delays in time-critical business matters. The lack of timely communication and failure to respect deadlines created unnecessary risk and operational disruption.

Professional representation requires respect for client authority, clear communication, and disciplined execution. Unfortunately, that was not my experience.

Based on these concerns, I cannot recommend this firm.

reviews.text: I've been working with an attorney in this office for over 9 weeks now on a straightforward procedural probate filing. Neither this attorney nor his paralegal are efficient or responsive. There are two-week gaps in communication while my timely responses and follow-up inquiries are ignored, it took 7 weeks for them to email the 3-page petition to me for notarization, and after I had it notarized immediately and returned to them, have now been ghosted yet again for another two weeks even after I inquired as to an update on its filing several days ago. This attorney has essentially been pre-paid for nine billable hours to get this completed and as of now I've lost 9 weeks of time in pursuing this critical financial matter. All I've gotten in response is apologies for the delays, blame placed on their new client information management system, and dismissals on my filing because he's "busy with other client matters." It stuns me to see all the 5-star reviews for this firm, as well as for the attorney I'm using, because that hasn't been my experience at all.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share your experience. We value all feedback as it helps us improve our services.

We’re sorry to hear that you’re dissatisfied with the handling of your probate matter. While we respectfully disagree with your characterization of the timeline and responsiveness, we understand that communication is critical and regret that you feel this has not met your expectations. It’s important to note that, to date, no payments have been made directly by you. As agreed, the billing for your matter will be processed through ARAG.

Our records indicate that we have been working diligently on your case, ensuring all necessary steps are taken to protect your interests. The procedural nature of probate can sometimes lead to delays that are outside our control, but we assure you that your case has been, and continues to be, a priority.

We would appreciate the opportunity to discuss your concerns further and clarify any misunderstandings. Please reach out to me directly at 435-635-7737 so we can ensure that any outstanding matters are resolved to your satisfaction. Please ask for Ben.

We take all feedback seriously and are committed to providing all our clients with the highest level of service.

I was hit by a car while riding my bicycle in the bike lane in the middle of the day. The crash left me with serious injuries and my bike a total loss. At first, I was referred to another attorney firm, but I quickly became frustrated—my calls and emails went unanswered, and I felt like no one was truly listening to me or fighting for me.

Thankfully, I had previously met the Advocate team at a local cycling event, and I reached out to them for help. From the very beginning, they treated me with dignity, respect, and genuine care. They listened, they communicated clearly, and they worked tirelessly on my behalf. Their professionalism and compassion made all the difference during a very difficult time in my life.

I am deeply grateful for their support in resolving my case, and I also want to thank them for all they do for the cycling community and the local events they sponsor. It means a lot to know my advocates truly care both inside and outside the courtroom.

I can’t recommend the Advocate team enough—thank you for standing up for me and for cyclists everywhere.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are so happy to hear about your positive experience, Mel. We are immensely grateful for your trust in our team during such a difficult time.

Anyone that gives this guy a 5 star is seriously ill. I had a court case that was extremely tone sensitive.  I paid the money to get the process going and then I heard nothing from his office for over a week.  When I finally called and asked for a progress report, someone in the office told me he was out on a family emergency.  I understand that things happen, but someone should've taken the reigns and continued the progress on my case.  When my court date finally came,  he showed up in hey dudes, looking completely exhausted.  We met for a few moments before our time in court, yet nothing we discussed mattered.  He went in the judges chambers with the opposing council and made a back door deal with them, prior to consulting me.  I left frustrated and defeated.  He did nothing but submit paperwork and take my money.  I could've done what he did, on my own.  Thanks but no thanks.  I'd rather go to court with no council than use this firm ever again.
Justin is truly delightful. He is calm and empathetic and has a quick grasp of all of the nuances of even really complicated situations. I highly recommend him.

I was very pleased with Justin.He is one of the most awesome attorneys I have ever met I couldn't have asked for a better attorney to represent me, this man Justin went above and beyond what an attorney would do for most people. LOOK NO FURTHER! I retained Justin Caplin for a very complicated divorce and child custody case. Justin is someone who truly has his clients best interest in mind; he will fight for you and most importantly he will intelligently analyze *your* personal situation and provide several scenarios that might give you a different outcome with each one. Thank you Stan.

I contracted with Stan Beutler to complete a QDRO in California.  He provided me with all the documents and detailed instructions to get the courts approval and file with the third party administrator.  The entire process took six months to complete from contacting Mr. Stan to receiving the disposition from PERS.  This had been hanging over me for 24 years and now it is resolved.  I highly recommend Mr. Stan, we had a verbal conversation where he answered all my questions and also responded to my emails when I had follow up questions.

I whole heartly recommend Attorney Stan Beutler! I was so nervous completing the QDRO, I kept putting it off, but he made the process so simple! The questions I had, he actually answered the phone, and helped me! He is knowledgeable, patient, and easy to speak with. Wouldn't it be wonderful if everyone had the same work ethics.

If I could give a zero I would!  Stan gave me such high hopes about helping me and the price was in my budget. I gave him everything he asked for and he dropped me like a hot potato with no reasons!  He didn’t even give me a referral; no explanation.  I’m wondering if when he heard my voice on the phone, he doesn’t want to help a black woman.  I’m reporting him to the Utah bar association for misconduct.  My son is a lawyer and he knows how to guide me through this situation.  Unfortunately he doesn’t do QDROs.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear MelanatedRN #reparations now:

After carefully reviewing your file, I respectfully declined to take your case, and I did not accept your payment. Your suggestion that I did not take your case because you are black, is baseless, hurtful, and hateful.

I believe that we are all children of a Heavenly Father and Mother who love each of us the same regardless of our race. I cherish his belief. When I was nineteen, I served a two-year mission in a third world country at my own expense assisting the wonderful people there, many of whom were black. I continue to serve many of my black brothers and sisters as a QDRO attorney.

Furthermore, I have family members with multiracial backgrounds, and I value my relationship with them.

I hope you find peace in your life!

reviews.text: I live in Alaska & was in the middle of a divorce in 2022. I called around to some law firms & the last document I needed to be finalized was a QDRO for my retirement, a bunch of numbers I didn’t understand looking at it. Most places asked for $7000 for a retainer. I did some research, found Mr. Stan, he was able to give me a guarantee finished QDRO with less than 10% of what Alaskan firms were asking for. There was only one small typo, which was sorted out with one simple phone call in just a short few minutes. I would recommend Stan to anyone needing a QDRO. Thanks!
About to contact Atty Beutler again...BUT WISH TO STATE THAT LAST TIME i contacted him was June 3, 2023 and he got back to me with paperwork June 5th 2023...I was amazed and greatful.

Do not hesitate to use Stan! One of the easiest and most helpful people during this long process of splitting things. Drew up our qdro perfectly and had 0 issues. The best part about using Stan is he continues to answer any questions you have afterwards about the process and getting it filed . Doesn’t leave you to figure it out on your own. Thanks again!
As described, no complaints here. Everything was taken care of in a timely manner and the QDRO did meet my state’s requirements for a QDRO. Stan answered my questions in a timely manner as well.

Attorney Stan is very knowledgeable. With his experience and professionalism, he was able to guide me through the process of preparing my QDRO. He's also very patient, I must add. I highly recommend his services to anyone.
Stan was very helpful and professional. He never delayed in any action or communication. I am so grateful to have worked with him!

Others seem to have had a great experience, so I cannot fathom why I was treated so poorly. I was referred to Stan Beutler by another law firm because I had some serious issues and questions trying to resolve my QDRO. I needed advice on what was going on because there were two 401(K) accounts involved, and if there was any paperwork or forms missing. Mr. Beutler apparently couldn't get me off the phone fast enough. When I told him the QDRO department at my ex's company had approved both QDROs, but they just needed to be signed by a judge—which I was having a hard time getting the court to do—I was curtly told he only drafted QDRO orders. When I tried to ask him if he would at least check the paperwork sent to me or offer advice on a course of action to get this finished (had no issue paying him), he practically hung up on me. I was literally in tears. I went through sheer utter hell, as my divorce and my mother dying unexpectedly were happening at the same time. Mr. Beutler made me feel hopeless and helpless all over again.
reviews.ownerResponse: Lori:  My apologies that my answer is not what you wanted to hear.  As we discussed, you should contact the person who drafted your two QDROs and ask them to complete what you paid them to do.  You should not have to pay another attorney to finish their work.  Thank you, anyway, for contacting me.  My condolences that your mother passed away.

reviews.text: I had a terrible divorce; I needed a Vanguard quadro drafted; but I just didn't want to deal with it! My ex's attorney tried to block the quadro by telling me that it was wrong, and I needed to pay him instead!  The judge was not happy with the other attorney's tactics and signed Mr. Beutler's quadro, no problem; it took vanguard about thirty days to process it; and they approved it without any changes; they sent me a letter explaining the tax consequences of my options and I had them mail me a check; many thanks to Mr. Beutler!

reviews.text: Aaron is a deeply committed and compassionate advocate. He truly cares that each of his worker's compensation clients get what they are owed. He doesn't waste time on stuff that doesn't matter, and he truly cares about making the process as painless as possible for his clients.

I have worked with Aaron on several cases involving injured workers. He is an excellent advocate for his clients and is very responsive. I highly recommend his firm for Utah injury and workers compensation matters.
Kurt rocks!

I was extremely disappointed with the service provided by Kurt A. Johnson, Esq. The executor hired him to provide a thorough accounting of the estate, but the experience has been anything but satisfactory.

First, the accounting records were incomplete and contained significant omissions. Notably, a $20,000 check (#213) and a $20,001.18 deposit were not included in the accounting. When questioned, Mr. Johnson asserted that these transactions could be omitted because they "nearly cancel each other out," which is fundamentally incorrect.

When I raised these issues, the response was not only dismissive but also unprofessional. Mr. Johnson proposed to charge $500 per hour to revise the accounting records, which is unreasonable given that the initial accounting was flawed. Additionally, he remarked, "I don’t understand why you are being so picky about very minor items, but I am happy to charge you additional fees to give you what you want."

As a beneficiary, I have the right to a clear and accurate understanding of the estate’s finances. Mr. Johnson's attitude and proposal to charge additional fees for correcting his own errors are unacceptable. This experience has raised serious concerns about the transparency and professionalism of Kurt A. Johnson, Esq.

I would not recommend Kurt A. Johnson, Esq. to anyone seeking reliable and professional accounting services, especially for sensitive matters such as estate management.
Kurt is great, bought car from estate and he's honest and a professional.

We have had the pleasure of working closely with the collections staff at Jenkins Bagley Sperry PLLC, and the experience has consistently been excellent.

Their team is incredibly helpful, knowledgeable, and responsive. In the world of collections—where situations can often be sensitive or difficult—they approach every matter with professionalism, reason, and a genuine willingness to help find fair and effective solutions.

Where most folks that end up in HOA collections don't fully understand the mechanics of HOA collections processes, the laws that govern them, their individual HOA governance processes, on top of each parties responsibilities - they take a brunt of the beating from Debtors- while always maintaining professionalism.

They take the time to explain processes clearly, provide practical guidance, and work collaboratively to ensure matters are handled properly and respectfully which makes a tremendous difference when in challenging situations. Thank you!

I have had the privilege of working with Jenkins Bagley Sperry, PLLC for nearly a decade now, and I cannot recommend their team highly enough. As someone who works closely with community associations and clients who are constantly navigating complex governance and legal matters, having a firm like Jenkins Bagley Sperry that prioritizes integrity and sound judgment 100% of the time is absolutely invaluable to me, the work that I do, and those that I serve.

They consistently provide clear, factual, and well-reasoned guidance, even when the right answer requires a more thoughtful path forward. They always ensure you are actually educated enough to make properly informed decisions in favor of the best interest of all parties involved.

Rather than simply arguing a case with a sole focus on profit without regard of the long-term consequences, I have gotten the opportunity to observe them time and time again assist my clients in reaching the most reasonable, sustainable, and legally sound outcome for the long run.

I’m grateful for the priceless guidance they have provided to both me and my clients over the years, and for the role they play in raising and maintaining standards across the entire industry.

Thank you all so much for all the work you do!
Great insight and not afraid to answer the difficult questions or at least define the guardrails.

Absolutely disgusting company cannot believe our HOA Is using them! Have two children with lots of medical conditions one who severely disabled so-called to see if I could pay half and then Pay half a month later they told me I could, but that I would have to pay an additional $800 if I wanted to do that and if I didn’t that they would put a lien on our property absolutely horrible company then they give you their website To pay on and charge you a huge fees on top of everything I already paid paid another almost $30 to process the payment!!  No human connections at all so ride and they just don’t care!!

Absolutely horrible human beings who refuse to give people any benefit of the doubt and will charge you insane amount of fees per day. Even with my own attorney and willing to pay original collection fee amount they still put a lien on my house. Do everything in your power to not deal with this greedy horrible company.. I just want to add an update on this review, it has now been almost a month since my account has been paid and this company has still not sent payment to my HOA . They took almost 1500 dollars in fees, they charged you for any communication you attempt with them to resolve your account. The only people this company benefits is HOA communities..

special thank you to Hope and her hard work and dedication to my case!

This is a complex case involving several legal question and deep discovery problems.   Mr. Pacini was proven to be a fierce and a strategic litigator who drove the case to a favorable outcome for me.

I continue to engage Mr Pacini on other matters following our hoa case.

Aaron Pacini with Jenkins Bagley Sperry has been a standout attorney for Orange Funding. His team is great, too.  Super pro and helpful.

From our first interaction, Aaron's ability to grasp the nuances of complex financial deals and apply sound legal judgment has been hugely valuable to me and the company.

I've worked with more attorneys than I can count in my career over the past 20+ years from many sides: my side of the table / underwriters / clients / sponsors, specialists, the list goes on forever when you're working with sophisticated parties in these often complex finance transactions.

Aaron operates with high level precision, balancing efficiency with thoroughness, and makes it clear that trust and fairness aren't just talking points. They drive his work.  He has added real value by cutting through the noise and giving Orange actionable, confident guidance.  In an industry where legal advice can be slow or vague, or sometimes feels like groups just trying to charge billable hours, big or small items, Aaron's responsiveness, reality based approach and clarity set him apart for both contract feedback and business strategies when outside unbiased legal eyes are absolutely needed.

reviews.text: Jenkins Bagley Sperry has done collection work for us for several years.  We have been very happy with their work.  Collections is not always pleasant, but working with the team at Jenkins Bagley Sperry has been.  We will continue to use them in the future.
Quinn Sperry has been a great addition to the team. Jenkins Bagley Sperry is a great law firm to work with for HOA related issues. Quinn is always very helpful when complicated HOA problems arise.

I have enjoyed working with several members of the Jenkins Bagley Sperry team for many HOA needs (collections, Governing Document/CC&R Amendments and clarification, etc). They are extremely knowledgeable and helpful for all things Homeowners/Owners Association related. I highly recommend!

Jenkins Bagley Sperry, PLLC Have been very helpful in removing the stress away from our Board Members of collecting past due home owners fees. Over the years, there have been many sleepless nights over this issue. Since joining with Jenkins Bagley Sperry, PLLC most of our delinquents accounts have been resolved allowing our HOA to focus on other issues with in our HOA. We would highly recommend them to all HOA's

I've worked with many HOA and collections attorneys, and I have learned what sets apart the great firms from the poor ones. Jenkins Bagley Sperry is a great firm. They are smart, fair, meticulous and polite even in the face of hostility, which is unfortunately common in HOA work.

When we raised a concern that their fees for collection were high, they listened to us closely, explained that they were being thorough, and they walked me through the careful process they follow to ensure that they do the best job they can for the HOA.

Jenkins Bagley and Sperry clearly did a lot of great work in preparing all of these cases that they analyzed with me and I appreciated their careful analysis, but I still wanted a solution with lower fees for our homeowners who are sent to collections. They came up a with a process that would allow them to still be very thorough but also reduce their fees dramatically. Not many law firms will do that.

It is pretty common for homeowners who fail to pay their dues to lash out at their management company and the attorneys, but what is not common is to find such professionals who are able to balance firm advocacy for the HOA with polite communication with people who are nothing short of mean-spirited -- Jenkins Bagley and Sperry is one of those rare groups of professionals.

Jenkins Bagley Sperry, PLLC has enabled our association to recover thousands of dollars in unpaid assessments and fines. They are professional, thorough and courteous in this process. Highly recommended!
They have been easy to work with in recovering money owed to our HOA.
We have proof that we are not late on our HOA payments and sent proof to the attorney and have not had any response.
Stop helping the HOA put liens on homes without any other notice. Absolutely stealing at that point from families that work hard. DO BETTER.

This is the first time I’ve actually dealt with a law firm that I would consider the stereotypical “shady lawyer”.
We thought that our HOA payments (of $20 a month) was on auto pay but for some reason it malfunctioned and we havent been paying for the last couple of months. Instead of notifying us by any means (literally no letter, no email, no phone call, nothing) we come home from church on a Sunday and we have a lien notice taped to our front door, open for the whole world to see. The next day we received SIX lien notices in the mail. So not only did we not hear anything about this late payment, the second that they decided to lien our house we begun to fee extremely harassed.
Our break down of what we owed:
$100 in HOA late fees
$670 in Lien fees. Yes, that’s right, $670 on a $100 balance. If that’s not despicable and so so shady, I don’t know what is.
Not to mention, if we had known ANYTHING about this balance we would have immediately paid.

When I emailed the law firm to explain what had happened I basically was told, “well, it’s not their responsibility to let you know if you have an over due balance.” I don’t know the law, maybe it’s not, but isn’t it common decency to at least reach out ONCE in some way before filing a $670 lien?!? (Though I’ve heard from many people there should have been an intent on o lien notice, which we obviously didn’t get.)

This is super shady business. I’m sure someone somewhere is making a lot of money off of us.

I honestly never thought the negative comments about lawyers were true until I learned about what this company is doing. They are helping an HOA cheat good people out of their money through a scam where people sign up for auto pay and then don’t take out the payments, blame them, and then put a lien on their home for not paying with absolutely no warning. Please boycott this company until they stop running this scam. There are honorable ways to make money. This has happened to multiple families in my area.

Greed! A few neighbors of mine had their autopay for HOA stopped without notice. These awesome people then hit them with a $600 lien for a missed payment. The HOA does nothing. Our neighborhood lawns are not taken care of. The websites calendar is 2 years behind. Collecting our money and running with it.

reviews.text:
Getting through a divorce most times can be a nasty event. Having used a number of attorneys in trying to get things done properly I learned of Graff Law. I wish I would have saved all that money and time by starting here.

Hands down the best in town.

I would recommend Graff Law Firm to anyone facing a divorce.  Jake and his associates were wonderful to work with.  Jake was scrupulously honest and forthright and helped me navigate an extremely difficult and drawn out divorce.  He worked tirelessly on my case and was the best advocate for me and my children that I could have hoped for.  He and his associates were very careful to keep track of the mountains of details in my case, took the time to fully understand and vet the many issues that needed to be addressed,  and worked closely with the other professionals needed to clarify the many financial questions that came up.  I am so grateful I had Jake and Graff Law Firm helping me through this incredibly difficult situation!

Graff Law firm has been so helpful and proactive throughout the whole process. They helped me feel calm and supported during a stressful season of life. Jake is very personable and always takes as much time as needed to listen and give all the best advice and support along the way! Highly recommend!
Jake represented my case with respect and compassion. He listened to my needs and put my kids’ best interest first in my divorce case.

This law firm is not only thorough and professional, but they are compassionate to the individual circumstances and hardships of their clients. I would not put my most important decisions and legal matters in the hands of anyone else.

I cannot express the utter gratitude and respect I have for Jake Graff and the Graff Law Firm.  Every person on his team made me feel supported and respected every step of the way, which was not easy as it was a long, drawn out and difficult divorce.  At one point the stress got to me so much that I fell very ill, and Jake asked if they could bring me meals or if there was anything I needed!  That is going so above and beyond anything you could ever expect.  Everyone there always took the time to answer any questions and make me feel heard and respected.  To summarize, the Graff Law Firm was a blessing in my life when I felt abandoned.  I have the highest respect for Jake Graff and his entire team!

Jake Graff is an outstanding, honest, and hard-working attorney. I had the privilege of working alongside Jake for over four years. I saw him tirelessly working on files at all hours, to ensure no detail was missed. He is a strategically-savvy, wise, and pragmatic attorney. If you have a family law matter, I strongly endorse Jake Graff.

BEWARE! He is the worst, most dishonest lawyer ever. He is a narcissistic person. He will take your money, and all you will get are lies, unreturned calls, and no information at all. We paid him over $23,000, to settle my mom’s estate. Over 9 months later we had not received any information and no money was distributed from the estate. With the help from another lawyer we were finally able to settle the estate ourselves in only 2 months. DO NOT HIRE JUSTIN HEIDIMEN!!!!!!!

I just spent the last 7 days in a St. George Court Room. I was represented by Justin and his team, Jaysha and Norm. I am the defendant in this case which has been active for about the past 13 years! The court proceedings, over the the last seven days have been like watching two Titans in a struggle, blow by blow as the attorneys presented and heard the evidence pertinent to each party. The plaintiffs attorney is a very competant and experienced attorney and a worthy opponent in the court room. I was very gratified as I observed Justin professionaly present and recieve critical information regarding the case, rapidly interpret the information and skillfuly develop supporting conclusions for our case. Justin's recall, association and compartmentalization abilities are stellar. The intricacies of this case are many with over 350 exibits! Remebering all of the exhibits and their importance and associations  require a memory of extreme depth. Justin's recall was instantaneous and accurate. His delivery is excellent and concise. He always demonstrated courtesy to the court protocal and the witnesses,. Justin always assisted me in understanding the relevance to all aspects of the conclusions arrived at by the plaintiffs and ourselves. Justin is supported in a court setting by Jaysha and Norm. These two really complete the professionalism and efficiency as they work hand in glove with Justin. Not only have I found exceptional skill and dedication in the Heideman Firm but also a display of character and integrity when the pressure of the case is so visible. If you need a Civil Attorney, your first call should be to the Heideman Law firm.

J. Stansfield

Justin Heideman is an awesome guy and as an attorney he is really best and supportive. I recommend him to all my friends. Take love Justin.
Yours Tahsin

My mother-in-law paid more than half the total, and they only completed about 10% of the work. Even so, they demanded full payment without showing any real progress. It's a waste of time and money. They basically stopped working on the case because we didn't want to keep paying without seeing any results.

Saraí was defensive when she spoke with her. To this day, we're still waiting for the documents, and they simply don't respond.

According to them, Michelle no longer works there, but when you have a major claim, she reappears, and then they go back to saying she doesn't work there. For them, this seems like a game, but for us, it's not.
So please, if you're reading this, you're still waiting for the documents you requested over a month ago.

reviews.text:
In 2021, LegalShield recommended Abraham Ivie from Witt Law. He was our attorney for what should have been an open-close custody case. However, because of his failing to provide a counterclaim to the original petition, as well as failing to apprise me of an appeal I could have made to the decision, my family is deeply suffering. He also did not provide the financial disclosures, or the initial disclosures at our hearing. He was difficult to get a hold of, and did not respond to most messages we were sending to use as evidence in our case.

I have had to find other representation to fix his mistake, and has cost me tens of thousands of dollars more than what I needed to spend. I have tried with new representation to fix his mistakes, and to no avail, 3 years later, we are nowhere closer to resolving this issue than when we started because the window to file those things closed.

I have attempted to reach out to their office multiple times to discuss, try to get my money back so that I may use that with new representation, and he has never responded. I will be making a report to the BAR and hopefully we can use THAT in court to repair the damage that has been done.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Ivie is no longer with the firm and we are not aware of the details of your complaint.  We are sorry that he was not able to help you. Most custody disputes are not, however, "open-close". Hopefully your new attorney has been able to help you to your satisfaction.

I don't think I can say anything good about it...

Greatest firm every i had jim as my lawer and absolutely love his work. Wont go anywhere else

Worst experience I’ve ever had with a law firm. Do yourself and big favor and move on to the next firm. It will have you the headache and pain of trying to deal with this unprofessional law firm.

They will not represent you or let you know what’s in your best interest. They simply take on a task to get paid and not represent you as a client. It’s all about the money for them.

If you feel the need to work with them avoid using Steven James “Jim” Park. He’s a nightmare and completely unprofessional.

Trust me, there are tons of law firms in St. George and Nevada to choose from. Avoid 1Law at all costs.

Jim Park at 1Law takes a personal approach to your case and has the utmost integrity. He works not just with his brain (which happens to be like a steel trap!) but with his heart.
I approached Jim regarding a car accident that my 16 yr old stepson was severely injured in a few months ago. Due to custody issues Jim informed me that I legally was unable to hire him. BUT...
Prior to learning that I wouldn't be able to formally hire Jim, he spent a few hours of his time listening to an extremely stressed out mother frantic for advice about what to do in regard to medical treatment and the insurance companies involved.
I avoided making several poor decisions based on Jim taking the FREE time out of his schedule to consult with me. Decisions that would have lead to financial problems in our future and worse, decisions that would not have allowed our son to get the medical treatment necessary to live a life as close to the one he had prior to the accident.

Hiring someone who is extremely knowledgeable, smart as a whip and happens to care about his clients is a GREAT FORCE to have on your side!

I highly recommend Jim Park with 1Law as his specialized knowledge, skills and experience in his field makes him the best!
Really helpful staff, and the receptionists are very nice ladies!
Best attorney in town

reviews.text: Recently, my husband and I had Thomas Crofts, with Saint George Estate Law in St. George, UT, do all of our estate documents (Trust, Wills, Advance Directives, Powers of Attorney, Warranty Deed to put our home in the name of the Trust - and several other docs as well) - and he did an excellent job! We're relatively new to St. George and did some research regarding estate attorneys and Thomas was highly recommended and very reasonably priced. reviews.text: My first contact was an amazingly friendly call from Bre at Saint George Estate Law, which was recommended by a friend at Sunriver.  And when I first went to the office, Bre greeted me with enthusiasm as she handed me a cold bottle of water on a typically hot day in St. George.  Shortly after, I met Thomas, who offered me my choice of seat in the conference room.  I had been depressed for months and had little energy for this in-person meeting, but by the end of our long, introductory conversation I was aware of my increase in energy from Thomas's empathic, intelligent demeanor that encouraged conversation.  Since then I have met with Thomas a few times as he has restated my trust, Durable Power of Attorney and Advanced Health Directive from California. While assuring me he wanted me to be completely comfortable with my trust and other documents, I decided to follow many of his valuable suggestions.  Thomas has also recommended other professionals to me and allowed them and me to meet in his office.  What a team Thomas and Bre make, and I can't recommend Saint George Estate Law enough!  Katharine

reviews.text: I am incredibly impressed with Thomas Crofts and his talented and attentive team at St George Estate Law. My husband's and my will was not an easy feat, and I am incredibly impressed by the tremendous work put into preparing an organized, detailed, thorough will. From beginning to end, Thomas (and Jennifer) did an awesome job. The first step was to sit down with Thomas and Jennifer, who discussed (at length) and in great detail exactly what my husband and I wanted to do with our estate, put in our will, and who are executor would be. We needed a lot of guidance, and we were really grateful to have the professional advise and knowledge of someone experienced in estate law, as well as real estate law, tax law, and marital law. We also sought advice about medical power of attorney and executorship, and were so appreciative of the advice we received. Thomas and Jennifer walked us through all the paper work, answered tirelessly all of our questions -- even by phone and not always by email -- and prepared a perfect document for us that we're incredibly happy with. After the document was prepared, we sat down with Thomas and Jennifer, who walked us through everything and explained in detail exactly what was prepared on our behalf. Jennifer is a notary, so we were able to sign all the documents and have them notarized there in the office. Everything was thoroughly explained to us, and we could not be happier with the service provided, at an affordable cost. SG Estate Law is the best in town!  Thank you, Thomas and Jennifer, for all of your hard work in helping us get the piece of mind that we need as we get older and really need to start planning for our future and for our family's future.

They did not communicate with me at all. I called to speak with an attorney and explained my need. They said they would get back to me by the end of day (Monday 6 hours till closing).
They did not.
A month went by and I decided to call them back to find out what was going on. They told me that they lost all the information about my situation, that I "Fell through the cracks". They again said they'd get in touch with me by the end of day. Again they didn't, for days. I called back at the end of the week and I was told they decided not to help me.
They didn't give me a reason for their decision.
They didn't communicate that to me in any way. Just left me hanging in the lurch.

I posted this negative review and all of a sudden they called me the next day after directly rejecting me, and then asked me to change my review of them.

So there. I changed my review.

Terrible way to conduct one's self. A law office with poor integrity.

I am fortunate to have had representation from the Snow Jensen & Reece team to assist in settling a family real estate dispute. SJR responded quickly and maintained an open line of communication throughout the entire settlement process. I highly recommend reaching out to the SJR team if you are ever in need of legal advice.

reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Sarah, I'm so sorry to hear you feel that way about us. We really like you, but please also keep in mind that you never were a client of ours. We'd really love to help you and to reach a resolution that you feel better about. I just called but missed you, please call me back so we can discuss. Your husband is our client. Therefore we are constrained by what he directs and we are on the same page with him and we are getting him well taken care of on his case. If you are having any misunderstandings with him please know that we would love to keep both of you happy ideally, because we are uninvolved in any miscommunication between the two of you and we have zero ill-will towards either of you. That said, communications between our client and us are confidential and we must keep them that way to honor the attorney-client relationship. Our client, your husband, is very happy with our representation. Our goal is always to handle personal injury cases as well as humanly possible for our clients and we work hard every day to do that. We wish you nothing but the best and, as I said, please feel free to call me and I am happy to discuss what details I can to try to help resolve the issue with you if at all possible and do what I can to take care of you and keep us all on good terms if possible.

reviews.text: Every time I call their phone service asks to leave a message and they'll get back to you.  I've left several messages over several weeks and have never been called back.  You should speak with your receptionists and make sure they're accountable for doing their jobs.  You missing out on clients who'd otherwise be willing to pay lots of money for your service.  I'll go elsewhere now.
reviews.ownerResponse: We’re sorry to hear about your experience and appreciate you bringing this to our attention. We reviewed our call and voicemail logs from the past few weeks but didn’t find any messages under your name or contact information. We strive to return all calls as quickly as possible, but as a busy immigration law firm, we sometimes miss returning calls to those who don't leave messages. Perhaps that is what happened here. If you’re still in need of assistance, we’d be happy to speak with you to see if we’re a good fit for your immigration needs. Please feel free to reach out again.

I notice a number of many other posts regarding Chad Rumsey's  professionalism and ability to deal with a number of different issues in his dealing with securing Green Cards for people .   Rather than repeating many of the same accolades  I would prefer to address my very specific issue that Chad ( I now call him Magic Man as a result ) was able to overcome and get me my coveted Green Card .  I am dealing with a severe health issue and that led Chad to find me a waiver from  all the requested vaccinations as my doctor issued a letter suggested by Chad where it would not be advisable to take these vaccinations .   His suggestion here  made all the difference and allowed to move forward in getting Immigration to agree and issue me my Green Card .  Chad saved my life in so  many ways that are hard to put into words !  If you have just normal requirements or need help with other issues like I had , Chad is clearly the Attorney to reach out to as he is clearly able to " think outside the box " and will surely find a way for you too to get your Green Card .  DON"T DELAY   !  Call Chad today !
Chad was extremely knowledgeable and attentive to our individual needs.  He has been responsive and helpful throughout the process of obtaining green cards.

reviews.text:
I am not a paid actor. I am neither a family friend nor acquaintance of the firm nor anyone within its employment. I am merely a client. This is my unbridled experience of Palmer Litigation, specifically, in working with "Billy" Palmer.

As a transplant to Utah, I had become accustomed to a variety of circumstances without even knowing it:

1. My social worth was equivalent to my income.
2. My role and rights as a father both are, and always will be, inferior to those of my children's mother.
3. My ethnic background is welcome so long as it poses no inconvenience to locals.

And it wasn't until after my divorce that I began to realize these implications. As my economic situation changed, being a single parent, my worth to those around me began declining too. In addition, despite massive criminal, legal and ethical violations against me from my co-parent (all of which being well-documented) she always, somehow, received the benefit of the doubt from the system at every turn. And, as an ethnic minority divorced from an ethnic non-minority, any presence of doubt has always left my civil rights and safety in question. Every time.

The only reason I mention any of this is not for judgement, sympathy, or even criticism. What I cannot emphasize enough, however, is how much NONE OF THIS mattered to Billy Palmer.

I consulted with Billy one day--same as any other attorney--and, as we ended our time together, he told me something that echoed in my mind: "if you need anything, call me."

I have heard people say this to me my entire life; friends family, church members and more with 90% of them never meaning it. "Call me if you need" has become a cliched maxim in my life, at least, tantamount to "good morning" or "have a nice day." So tell me why, when Billy said this, I could feel in my soul that he meant it.

Less than one week later, unplanned and unexpected, I needed help. I had reached out to everyone I could to no avail. And, as I was prepared to give up, Billy's words echoed through my mind. Against hope, I called him; his secretary picked up immediately and patched me through to his cell, as he was driving.

If you're reading this, I cannot emphasize enough: I am nobody. I am not a high profile case nor client. I am not a high roller or VIP. My resources and impact on this world will have been virtually nothing if I were to leave this world today. Why did his secretary pick up immediately? Why did she forward me to his cell without hesitation? Why did Billy take the call while he was ON THE ROAD? My mind still can't comprehend these questions.

But he did. Billy is now representing me on multiple fronts and, every time I ask him how I can show my gratitude, all he tells me is "let people know I care." So here I am.

Billy is not just an unequivocal attorney--he is his brother's keeper. I have no idea what I did in life to deserve the benefit of Billy's support but I can guarantee you that I will spend the rest of my life singing his praises because he has changed the trajectory and nature of mine for perpetuity.

This is a long review. I don't care. Hire Billy, let him represent you, watch him change your life. Simple as that. And, if you have any doubt as to the purpose or nature of my review, contact me: I will repeat my experience and this story to you happily in person, any day of the week.

Thanks Billy.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your heartfelt review. We're truly honored to hear how much Billy's dedication impacted your life. Your words beautifully capture what we strive for at Palmer Litigation – caring beyond just a case.

reviews.text:
We’re all aware how corrupt the judicial system is in most reguards which has led me in my mind to not trust anyone associated with it, including lawyers cause they communicate with prosecutors and judges all day everyday. So in my mind they live in a I scratch your back you scratch mine world. which has led me to doubt them all.

I learned in this process with Palmer litigation that, that was a complete false perspective. He had my back from the beginning til the end… There was a point in time more toward the beginning of the process where I tried reaching out to Billy excessively cause how nervous I was and I had tons of questions and I didn’t get the responses quick enough. (I then had to realize he’s a busy man, I’m not his only client) but while I was sitting with no responses,  I got extremely paranoid and I began judging, wondering if he was on my side or not. Come to realize that was my own issue.

Billy was genuinely here  to protect me, keep my case confidential and do everything in his power to reduce or get my charges dropped to something less. And that’s EXACTLY what he did and am so grateful for his time and energy in doing that for me and my family.

CHARGES FROM THE POLICE
1 assault (for pushing my girlfriend away from me while she was attacking me)
2 domestic violence in the presence of a child (x3)
3 hanging up on the police
4 intoxication

CHARGES AFTER PALMER LITIGATION HANDLED MY CASE
(Plea in obeyance)
1 disorderly conduct
2 intoxication

Aaron and his team are fantastic - they are so helpful and really take the time to get to know the ins and outs of your case.  They have fought for us and continue to fight for us - we really appreciate their help!

Mr. Prisbrey and his team are nothing short of truly EXCEPTIONAL and  AMAZING.  He and his team go above and beyond in their dedication navigating the complexities of a really ugly personal injury and workers compensation situation, working around the clock as my advocates.  In a situation where I battled surgery after surgery and was beyond exasperated with my previous employer's avoidance in paying my bills, refusing to financially compensate me for lost wages, Mr. Prisbrey and his team were my bulldogs, fighting and defending me the entire way, doing everything in their power to get me the financial and medical support I needed.

I cannot speak highly enough of their casework, dedication to my case, personal emotional support for me and my family, exceptionally high personal and work ethic, and meticulous management of my case.  His paralegal team are superb at keeping books and sorting through all my thousands of pages of messy medical records while emotionally supporting me through one of the hardest times in my life.  I always had a shoulder to cry on, a hand to help me through tough times, and a smile to remind me that there was a light at the end of this tunnel, and that it wasn't a train coming at me!!

If you think you may have a situation involving personal injury or workers compensation, to not consider Mr. Prisbrey would be doing yourself a CONSIDERABLE disfavor.  He was able to get me a settlement that I didn't even know I was entitled to, and to help me secure financial stability while I heal from my substantial injuries.

GOD BLESS AARON PRISBREY AND HIS TEAM!

reviews.text: I supported my step sister (with disabilities) in hiring Travis for a special needs trust, paying $2,000 with the promise we’d get everything we needed to fund it quickly so she could apply for much needed Medical Assistance . That didn’t happen. The intial appointment was acceptable-though they didn't include me as we all agreed when setting up the appointment- I waited for an hour after the receptionist told me they'd call me into the meeting. Additionally, the trust’s EIN was never provided, and after weeks of ignored calls and messages, we only got a response after involving the BBB and Utah State Bar. We had to find the EIN elsewhere ourselves. This kind of delay could seriously harm disabled individuals who don’t have advocates. While we did receive a partial $500 refund (after lots of pushing for it), this experience leaves me questioning his communication, reliability, and expertise. It was a stressful and less than satisfactory experience. Families should be cautious.

I had a deeply disappointing experience with Boyack Christiansen Legal Solutions during my divorce. From the very beginning, it felt like I had no one truly advocating for me. Despite the fact that there were no kids involved, and my ex had her own business — a business I helped build by putting her through college, paying her business taxes, and funding her overhead costs — she still walked away with all the proceeds from the house sale.

What’s worse, I had to do most of the negotiating myself. My lawyer seemed disinterested and unmotivated. I was the one who fought to reduce the alimony (indentured servitude) from 20 years to 10. Meanwhile, my ex had a legal team that was clearly fighting for her every step of the way. The imbalance was undeniable.

The situation only got worse. I was falsely accused of cheating — a blatant tactic used for leverage — and found myself surrounded by females who showed no interest in challenging those claims. I had no male representation, and it felt like my voice was ignored.

If you’re looking for a law firm that will fight for you, I strongly urge you to look elsewhere. Boyack Christiansen Legal Solutions left me feeling isolated, unsupported, and ultimately worse off than I should have been. I would not recommend them to anyone in need of real advocacy.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are truly sorry to hear about your negative experience, Aeren. Your concerns highlight important issues that we need to address, and we appreciate you bringing them to our attention. It’s disappointing to learn that you felt unsupported during such a critical time. We will investigate this situation to ensure our team provides better advocacy and support to all our clients in the future. Thank you for your feedback; it is essential for our improvement. The tough thing is we have done a search and can find no client by this name.

I cannot say enough about the way Mr. Fisher handled my case. I started out with another attorney, Billy Palmer from Palmer litigation. After several weeks of no contact from him, and not answering any of my calls I contacted his office and told them that I do not want him to represent me anymore. Later that day he called me back and spoke to me for quite some time asking me why I did not want to retain him anymore, desperately trying to plead his case for me to continue with his representation.  I was charged a great amount of money for what he said was him going through discovery. When in all actuality he told me the reason he hadn't contacted me because he had not been able to go through discovery because he had just received it and had been very busy. So I was charged for him to go through my discovery which he stated himself he had not had the time to do so.  So I did a little more research and came in contact with Fisher litigation. Mr Fisher spoke to my wife and I for 2 hours giving us his insight into my case and guiding us in the proper direction. He was completely open and honest, and upfront. No promises, just facts.  My wife and I decided we would hire him. We contacted him several times a day day in and day out about the case. I am sure he must have gotten  very annoyed at our constant calls and text messages. From day one he treated us with professionalism and kindness. He worked extremely hard on my case. My case was a first degree aggravated assault. 15 years to life in prison. ( Which l did not do). Mr. Fisher worked tirelessly on my case and was able to get it dismissed even before my trial began. He showed the prosecutor  the facts he had uncovered through his hard work. I am in very poor health and would not have survived in prison due to my illness. I owe Mr. Fisher my life. I much more than  highly recommend Fisher legal. I haven't the words to thank him enough. Cory Hatch

reviews.text:
When I first met with Pete Stack, he presented himself as a strong, aggressive advocate who would fight for my interests. On the day of mediation, I initially felt confident having him there. However, as the session progressed, it became clear he was unprepared and had done little to set me up for success.

Throughout the process, he allowed opposing counsel to dominate the conversation without effective pushback. When I faced difficult, time-sensitive decisions in a highly stressful situation, he offered no clear guidance or professional opinion to help me navigate them. In some instances, when opposing counsel presented information, he seemed unaware of the details being discussed.

Given the significant legal fees I paid, I left feeling extremely let down. The outcome of that day felt like losing twice — in the case itself and in the representation I received.

Worth every penny!

Pete Stack was an amazing advocate for my divorce. He treated both me and my ex with genuine concern and dignity, which helped set a respectful tone throughout the process. He brought strong expertise and gave thoughtful advice, even for my most complicated questions. He built trust and rapport early on and consistently proved his credibility.

The whole team at Turner Law has been great to work with. I highly recommend them.

reviews.text: At 81 years old I have been around a little bit and like to think I have a good sense of who does a good job for you and who does not. Fortunately we connected with the right law firm to handle legal matters for a just financial compensation. That Law Firm is WE WIN INJURY LAW of St George Utah.   Our interaction with Jamilex, Tyler and Jaren of WE WIN was of the highest professional order. They demonstrated a genuine concern for our needs with a desire to achieve the best outcome possible.  During the process their response to questions was always timely, informative and compassionate to alleviate our concerns. In particular the mediation meeting was especially impressive with Tyler and Jaren's strategic approach to gain the best possible outcome for my son, of which they accomplished.  I would not hesitate for a second to recommend this Law Firm to anyone in need of injury representation.
reviews.ownerResponse:
John, thank you for your kind review! Helping your son meant a great deal to our entire team. We saw firsthand the strength, love, and resilience you’ve shown through such a difficult experience, and we’re deeply grateful for the trust you placed in us to fight for him.

We’re so glad that justice was served and that your son’s future is more secure because of it. Clients like you remind us why we do what we do every day, with compassion, persistence, and heart. From all of us at We Win Injury Law, thank you for allowing us to be part of your family’s journey.

He relieved all worries and with his team, handled all the medical details so we could focus on healing. He communicated constantly so we were always up to speed on the next step. In the end, Tyler secured a very high value settlement.  If in need of similar care, make "The Best Call Ever".  5 stars in no where near enough.

reviews.text: Colby is awesome!

I was clear from the first meeting what my priorities were in my divorce and Robert took that to heart when representing me throughout the divorce process.  The ultimate settlement reflected what was important to me and what didn’t really matter too much.  I’m sure everyone that goes through divorce feels like they either conceded too much or didn’t extract enough.  At the end of the day, my goals were to maintain custody of my kids (joint) and to protect my financial future.   Robert was instrumental in making sure these goals were met and my legal fees were money well spent!

Additionally, I gained a new respect for family law lawyers (my now ex-wife’s too).  They’re dealing with people going through the worst time of their lives and emotions are always high.  The ability that Robert (and my ex-wife’s lawyer) had to navigate those emotions and help arrive at a fair settlement without extracting unnecessary legal fees was impressive and very much appreciated.

reviews.text:
I cannot say enough good things about Swenson and Shelley. They represented me after a devastating accident in May 2024 when a motorcycle traveling at a very high speed missed a hairpin turn, struck my car, and the young rider tragically lost his life. I was not at fault, but the emotional and physical toll on me was overwhelming.

As a former home and hospice care worker, I knew immediately how serious the situation was, and the trauma stayed with me. It was just days before Mother’s Day, and my heart was broken for the young man’s mother. I suffered PTSD, a shoulder injury, whiplash, and required ongoing treatment and physical therapy. Because of my injuries, I was no longer able to continue my job as a postal worker and had to resign.

From the very beginning, everyone at Swenson and Shelley treated me with compassion, patience, and respect. They truly listened as I grieved and recalled the accident, never making me feel rushed or dismissed. Every person I spoke with was professional, kind, and supportive during one of the most difficult times of my life.

I was very pleased with the outcome of my case and the settlement they secured for me. More importantly, I felt genuinely cared for—not just as a client, but as a person. I am deeply grateful to Swenson and Shelley for their professionalism, empathy, and dedication, and I highly recommend them to anyone in need of a personal injury attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your experience! We're so glad to hear that Mr. Jeffery Lopez made you feel heard and informed during your first interaction with us. It's our priority to ensure clients feel comfortable and educated throughout the process. We appreciate your trust in Swenson and Shelley Injury Attorneys, and we look forward to continuing to support you every step of the way!

reviews.text:
I want to express my heartfelt appreciation and give the highest recommendation for Swenson & Shelley. What began as an incredibly stressful and uncertain period in my life became much more manageable thanks to the professionalism, dedication, and compassion of the entire team. From my first interaction with Chrissy and the intake specialist, I felt heard, supported, and cared for. They clearly explained every step of the process and consistently maintained transparent and timely communication. I was never rushed into making a decision I wasn’t comfortable with. The empathy from the team made all the difference during such a difficult time. Navigating a legal process especially after suffering several severe injuries is never easy, but the team went above and beyond to lift that weight off my shoulders.

I want to especially highlight the exceptional work of the paralegal, Chrissy, whose guidance, responsiveness, and meticulous work provided a steady anchor throughout this process. Chrissy worked methodically and was super efficient with gathering all my medical records and bills together. Her attention to detail is unmatched as she found and saved me from 50k missing in my medical bills which had been overlooked by my previous injury lawyers. Chrissy is of the highest caliber and her outstanding work deserves recognition for the all star that she is.

I am grateful for the efforts of Swenson & Shelley. My case was favorably resolved and I can finally move forward with peace of mind. I am so appreciative for everything they did-for the seamless way my case was handled, for their patience with me in explaining matters, and for their expertise and hard work. I recommend this law office highly to anyone in need of legal advice after an injury. I am truly grateful to have had Swenson & Shelley to represent me during this incredibly difficult time.

Respectfully,
Kirk W.

We’re thrilled that Chrissy and the entire team were able to provide the care, guidance, and expertise you needed. Brian and Kevin are the best in the field! When we first encountered each other Brian promised me that Swenson and Shelley wouldn't settle unless all of my concerns were addressed. We never settled even when the defense was making serious offers. My case ended in arbitration double the policy limits. I truly believe if I had chosen another lawyer to represent me, they would have taken the serious offers made right before arbitration. When the defense saw Swenson and Shelley as my legal representatives, they knew they would have to pay policy limits with one heck of a fight.  Brian and Kevin gave me and my case personal attention, and their legal advice was spot on.
I would give them six stars if possible. I entered a legal agreement with Brian and Kevin as my representation, but have left with two lifelong friends. They act with respect and integrity, and I recommend them to any person I encounter who needs representation in an injury case.

reviews.text:
Five stars for Smart Law, no question about it.

I’ve been in accidents before, but nothing like this. After the crash that left me paralyzed, I didn’t even know where to begin. Everything felt upside down—physically, emotionally, financially. But Smart Law stepped in and made sure I didn’t have to face it alone.

They didn’t treat me like a number or a file on a desk. They treated me like a person whose life had been flipped. They knew what to go after, what mattered long-term, and how to navigate a situation that felt impossible. They understood the difference between being hurt and being changed, and they fought for that difference.

They made sure I got what I needed: equipment, care, therapy, everything. They understood the process, explained it clearly, and stayed in touch the whole way. I could focus on learning to walk again, with crutches for now, while they handled the heavy legal stuff. That kind of peace of mind is hard to put into words.

I’m beyond thankful for the way they showed up for me and my family. If you’re dealing with a life-changing injury and don’t know where to turn, start with Smart Law. They’ll have your back.
reviews.ownerResponse:
James, thank you so much for sharing your experience!

I loved what you said about the difference between being hurt and being changed. Car accidents are never fun, but it becomes a truly life changing event when the resulting injuries are as serious as yours. It becomes not just about recovering compensation for medical bills and property damages, but providing the needed support to pick up the pieces of your life and move forward in the best way possible. We admire your optimism through this process, and are grateful you allowed us to be part of your team.

Best of luck going forward!

reviews.text:
My Experience with Injury Smart Law – Highly Recommended!

In 2021, I was in an auto accident that injured me, totaled my car, and wasn’t my fault. My dad reached out to a family friend, Attorney Barry Clarkson, who referred me to Brad Harr at Injury Smart Law. I called them and got an appointment the very next day.

Brad took the time to explain how he and his team could help me, and I immediately felt confident they’d take great care of me. Then, 17 months later, I was in another accident—again, not my fault—leaving me injured and without a car. Without hesitation, I hired Brad to handle this case as well.

Brad Harr, Jed Harr, Laura Irvine, and Lisa Loris have been incredible to work with. They’re excellent communicators, always answering my questions and addressing my concerns. The entire team treated me like family, going above and beyond to support me during a difficult time.

Because of my injuries—some of which are permanent—I faced mounting medical bills, totaled cars that needed replacing, and overwhelming paperwork. Brad and Jed ensured I received full value for my vehicles and that my rental expenses were covered. Laura coordinated with my medical providers, making sure my treatments continued without upfront costs by placing charges on an auto accident lien after PIP ran out.

Brad secured a fair settlement for my second accident, despite the complexity of having two cases so close together. (The first accident is still pending.)

I can’t say enough good things about this team, especially Laura Irvine. She kept me informed, answered every question (or got answers quickly), and always returned my calls the same day. Over the past four years, she’s even become a friend.

If you need a personal injury attorney, I highly recommend Injury Smart Law—Brad Harr, Jed Harr, Laura Irvine, and Lisa Loris. They’ll treat you with respect, compassion, and fight to get you the best possible outcome.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for a thoughtful review. We are not left speechless very often.
We’re sorry that you’ve had to use our services at all, but we look forward to continuing to represent you in your other case.

reviews.text: Brad and Jed are seriously the BEST of the best. Super professional and thorough. They are fair and do what is right. We really hesitated to get a lawyer, it just feels weird to us. We tried to handle the accident and insurance ourselves, but we quickly realized that the game is rigged. Insurance agencies have many lawyers working for them, not you. They bank on you just taking whatever they offer, even if it isn't enough to cover your costs or fair. It's frustrating. We almost didn't get a good settlement because valid info was being disregarded, but Jed really fought to tell our story and made some great points that couldn't be denied. I HIGHLY suggest getting a lawyer to handle all the phone calls and paperwork that needs to be done. It feels great to have someone in your corner looking out for your best interest. Injury Smart Law does a great job communicating what is happening and they make sure they know what the desired outcome is for you. If you are thinking you need a lawyer, you do, and Brad and Jed are exactly who you need.
